# Repository Settings — Apply on GitHub

> **Internal only.** This file is **not** linked from the README and lives in `.github/` so it never appears in a recruiter's browsing path. Use it as a copy-paste source when configuring the repository on github.com.

> GitHub → your repo → **About** (cog icon, top right) → paste **Description** and add **Topics** below. Save.

---

## Repository Name (recommended)

`comptia-cloud-plus-cv0-004-study`

---

## Description (copy exactly — 350 char max)

```text
CompTIA Cloud+ CV0-004 study notes — Domain 1 complete (Cloud Architecture, IaaS/PaaS/SaaS/FaaS, shared responsibility, HA/DR, networking, containers, Kubernetes, cost, databases), mapped to AWS, Azure, and GCP. Includes Mermaid diagrams, cheatsheets, flashcards, and practice questions. Built for the exam and for recruiters.
```

> **Why this version:**
> - Leads with the **cert name + code** (exact-match search).
> - Lists **specific technologies** recruiters keyword-search.
> - Names the **3 hyperscalers** (AWS / Azure / GCP) explicitly.
> - Mentions **deliverables** (diagrams, cheatsheets, flashcards, Qs) — proves depth.
> - Ends with a **dual-purpose hook** ("for the exam and for recruiters") — signals intent.

---

## Topics (add all — 20 max)

```text
comptia
cloud-plus
cv0-004
cloud-computing
aws
azure
google-cloud
kubernetes
terraform
docker
devops
infrastructure-as-code
networking
disaster-recovery
high-availability
cloud-security
cloud-architecture
containers
certification
study-notes
```

> **Why this list:**
> - `comptia`, `cloud-plus`, `cv0-004`, `certification` → recruiter search hits.
> - `aws`, `azure`, `google-cloud`, `terraform`, `kubernetes`, `docker`, `containers` → tech-stack search.
> - `cloud-architecture`, `cloud-security`, `disaster-recovery`, `high-availability`, `networking` → role-skill search.
> - `devops`, `infrastructure-as-code` → adjacent role signal.
> - `study-notes` → captures the "active learner" intent for candidate searches.

---

## Website (optional)

Leave blank, or set to your portfolio URL once you have one.

## Social Preview (recommended)

- Path: `.github/social-preview.png` (1280 × 640)
- Create in Canva / Figma with: cert name, "Domain 1 ✅ Complete", a small shared-responsibility diagram, AWS / Azure / GCP logos, your GitHub handle.
- GitHub picks this up automatically from `.github/`.

## Releases / Releases Only

Tag stable points and write a short Release:
- `v1.0.0` — Domain 1 complete
- `v1.1.0` — Portfolio restructure + study aids
- `v2.0.0` — All 5 domains complete

## Features to disable (not relevant for a docs repo)

- [x] **Wikis** — off (the repo IS the wiki)
- [x] **Issues** — on (for typo / inaccuracy reports)
- [x] **Discussions** — on (optional, builds community)
- [x] **Projects** — on (track Domain 2–5 progress)
- [x] **Sponsorship** — off
- [x] **Preserve this repository** — on (Archive button)

---

## Profile README snippet (for `<user>/<user>` repo)

```markdown
## 📘 Currently studying
- [CompTIA Cloud+ CV0-004](https://github.com/<user>/comptia-cloud-plus-cv0-004-study) — Domain 1 ✅ complete
```

This pins the cert prep to your GitHub identity.
